subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: 2nd pass (2026-05-28, bias-corrected): Russian-EXILE (Meduza/Riga), European, IMF, and OSINT (ISW/DeepState) confirm the war is ACTIVE and talks failed (only POW swaps, no settlement). The exile read frames it as deadlocked, not momentum. EU accession is ONE cluster (Fundamentals) opening ~June 16, not a full unblock - rule_of_law near nudged to -1.

Live-data refresh (2026-05-28): factors re-validated against diverse, independent sources (multilateral bodies + non-US/non-Anglophone press; state-self-reported data still excluded). Notes mark what moved.
v2.0 source-discipline re-run applied; the State Statistics Service is operational and relied upon by the IMF/World Bank (no entanglement discount), with documented data gaps in occupied areas.

Trade re-weighted 0.05 -> 0.17: Black Sea export disruption near-term, but EU integration + reconstruction + agricultural/critical-mineral export capacity is a large long-term positive-skew trade opportunity.
HIGH-VARIANCE / POSITIVE-SKEW case: wide confidence intervals; the outcome depends heavily on war resolution.
War must end on any 5-10y horizon; the trajectory is potentially strongly positive versus the US's near-certain negative drift.
Loss-asymmetry caveat: conscription/mortality/displacement tail risk is catastrophic for an individual and deserves weight beyond probability.
Slant-balance audit: the prior input set leaned pro-Ukraine/Western; balanced by holding the fiscal catastrophe (-7/-8) and demographic damage at full weight and keeping near-term conflict at -10.

economic.notes: Catastrophic wartime fiscal state offset by remarkably well-managed monetary/banking institutions and a large asymmetric upside (resources, EU accession, reconstruction, strategic manufacturing/logistics). Trade re-weight strengthens the long-term positive-skew read.

fiscal_state.notes: IMF EFF approved Feb 2026 ($8.1B, part of a $136.5B 4-yr package); 2026 financing gap ~$52B filled via ERA/EU/G7/WB; 2026-27 residual ~$63B. Program now formalized (was pending) - confidence raised, score unchanged (catastrophic but financed).

monetary_independence.notes: NBU strong independence even in wartime; hryvnia stability managed; inflation contained - an institutional success story.

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: Hryvnia not a reserve currency; neutral.

inflation.notes: ~1% m/m early 2026; well-managed despite war; energy-strike supply pressure.

banking_stability.notes: Operational throughout the war (remarkable); NBU supervision maintained; capital controls necessary but distorting.

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Black Sea export corridors disrupted near-term, but EU accession, Association Agreement integration, reconstruction demand, and large agricultural + critical-mineral export capacity make this a major long-term positive-skew trade opportunity. Resource-rich but war-gated: the upside is real and contingent on resolution.

institutional.notes: Weak pre-war baseline but a sharply positive reform trajectory under EU-accession pressure. Neutrality masks divergence: markedly better institutions possible in 10y if reforms continue.

rule_of_law.notes: EU-accession step is concrete but modest: Commission proposes opening only Cluster 1 ('Fundamentals') ~June 16 2026 (new Hungarian PM Magyar negotiated a single-cluster opening, not a full unblock). Near nudged -2 -> -1; long +2 holds. Anti-corruption reforms continue under martial law.

statistical_integrity.notes: State Statistics Service operational and credible; IMF/WB rely on it; some war disruption in occupied areas.

civil_service_capacity.notes: Reform-oriented with Western advisors; EU-driven modernization; brain drain and mobilization reduce capacity near-term.

press_freedom.notes: Far stronger than Russia/pre-2014; diverse media; wartime restrictions (unified telethon) within a legitimate-exception frame.

political_social.notes: Dominated by active warfare near-term; normalizes substantially post-war.

political_stability.notes: Wartime governance; elections suspended (constitutional under martial law); uncertain post-war transition.

civil_liberties.notes: Martial-law restrictions; conscription affects males 18-60 with exit limits; war-justified surveillance/censorship.

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Actively welcomes foreign skilled workers, esp. reconstruction-relevant; nominally welcoming but a country at war.

social_violence.notes: Active warfare; energy-system attacks; civilian casualties; mandatory evacuations; elevated mortality risk.

demographics.notes: 6-8M refugees abroad; hundreds of thousands of casualties; effective population sharply reduced; uncertain refugee return.

geopolitical.notes: Maximum conflict exposure near-term (active war); post-war could be a NATO-aligned, EU-integrated, security-guaranteed state.

alliance_reliability.notes: Strong EU/UK/Canada/Nordic support; unreliable US support under the current administration; EU accession proceeding; cannot self-defend without external support.

conflict_involvement.notes: War STILL ACTIVE as of May 2026: front largely static (ISW/OSINT), localized Russian pushes near Pokrovsk, no breakthrough. Heavy Trump-brokered diplomacy (May truce, Istanbul talks) produced only POW swaps - no settlement; Russia 'has not budged.' Hold near -10; confidence up on OSINT corroboration.

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Martial-law capital controls; conversion/remittance restrictions; lifted post-war.

physical_practical.notes: Severe war damage near-term; reconstruction with EU funding could yield modern infrastructure long-term (post-WWII analogy).

climate.notes: Moderate; strong agricultural potential; war environmental damage recoverable.

healthcare.notes: War-stressed; facilities damaged; medical brain drain; Western-supported critical functions.

infrastructure.notes: Winter 2025-26 grid damage severe (waves of 300+ drones/30+ missiles; thermal & hydro bombed out, surviving on nuclear; rolling 3-4hr blackouts). ~$588B reconstruction need; long-term rebuild upside. Worse-confirmed; confidence up (UN, ISW).

crime_safety.notes: Conventional crime relatively low; war violence is the dominant safety factor (counted in social_violence).

disaster_insurance.notes: War damage uninsurable; property insurance non-functional in affected areas; low quake risk.

summary: A high-variance, positive-skew case. Near-term factors are dominated by an active war - catastrophic on conflict, fiscal, infrastructure, and demographics - but the long-horizon trajectory points up: a war that must eventually end, an EU-accession path now unblocking, large reconstruction demand, rich agricultural and mineral export capacity, and monetary institutions that have held up under wartime stress. Assets are genuine high-risk/high-return and the hryvnia's downside is limited short of losing the war. The catastrophic individual tail risks (conscription, mortality, displacement) deserve weight beyond their probability.
